# Red Flags by Signal Layer

| Layer | Red flags |
|---|---|
| Visibility | synchronized posting, repeated captions, shared media, sudden spikes then flatlines, many accounts with one subject, same asset across platforms |
| Credibility | hidden incentives, edited reviews, generic testimonials, review bursts, reviewer accounts with thin histories, fake follower demographics |
| Conversion | cheap leads with low qualification, repeated form fields, impossible geography, high clicks but no pipeline, low trial activation |
| Attribution | abnormal click-to-install timing, high last-click capture, attribution source mismatch, organic traffic suddenly shrinking, installs with poor retention |
| Legitimacy | vague ARR definitions, launch rank without retention, customer logos without case studies, compliance claims without audit trail, waitlist size without activation |

## Research caution

A red flag is not proof. Red flags identify where to ask better questions, collect more evidence, and compare against baselines.
